questions.q: In which present-day Indian state is the Konark Sun Temple located?
questions.explanation: The Konark Sun Temple stands at Konark in the eastern state of Odisha, near the Bay of Bengal.

questions.q: To which Hindu deity is the Konark Sun Temple dedicated?
questions.explanation: The temple is dedicated to Surya, the Sun god, and is conceived as his celestial chariot.

questions.q: The Konark temple is famously designed in the form of what kind of object?
questions.explanation: The temple is built as a colossal stone chariot of the Sun god, complete with wheels and horses.

questions.q: How many pairs of carved wheels adorn the base of the Konark chariot?
questions.explanation: The chariot has twelve pairs of intricately carved stone wheels along its base.

questions.q: How many horses are depicted as drawing the chariot of the Sun at Konark?
questions.explanation: Seven horses are shown pulling the chariot, symbolizing the seven days of the week.

questions.q: In which century was the Konark Sun Temple built?
questions.explanation: The temple was constructed in the 13th century CE, around 1250.

questions.q: What is the primary building material of the Konark Sun Temple?
questions.explanation: The temple is built chiefly of stone, including khondalite, laterite and chlorite.

questions.q: What international recognition does the Konark Sun Temple hold?
options: New Seven Wonders of the World
questions.explanation: The Konark Sun Temple was inscribed as a UNESCO World Heritage Site in 1984.

questions.q: Near which large body of water does the Konark Sun Temple stand?
questions.explanation: Konark lies on the Odisha coast near the Bay of Bengal.

questions.q: The Konark Sun Temple is sometimes referred to by what nickname among Europeans?
options: The Red Fort of the Sun
questions.explanation: European sailors called it the Black Pagoda, in contrast to the white Jagannath temple at Puri.

questions.q: Which king is credited with building the Konark Sun Temple?
questions.explanation: King Narasimhadeva I of the Eastern Ganga dynasty built the temple around 1250 CE.

questions.q: To which dynasty did the builder of the Konark Sun Temple belong?
questions.explanation: Narasimhadeva I belonged to the Eastern Ganga dynasty that ruled Kalinga (Odisha).

questions.q: To which broad regional school of temple architecture does Konark belong?
questions.explanation: The temple is a masterpiece of the Kalinga school of Odishan temple architecture.

questions.q: The twelve pairs of wheels at Konark are widely interpreted as symbolizing what?
options: The twelve months of the year
questions.explanation: The twelve pairs of wheels are commonly read as the twelve months, and they can function as sundials.

questions.q: What practical timekeeping function are the carved wheels of Konark believed to serve?
options: Sundials that tell the time of day
questions.explanation: The intricately carved wheels act as sundials, their spokes and rim markings indicating the time.

questions.q: What is the name of the grand assembly or dance hall in front of the main temple at Konark?
questions.explanation: The detached pillared hall in front is the Nata Mandira, or dance hall, richly carved with dancers.

questions.q: What is the towering structure (now collapsed) that once rose over the sanctum called in Odishan architecture?
questions.explanation: The main tower over the sanctum is the deul (shikhara), which is believed to have risen well over 60 metres before collapsing.

questions.q: Which large surviving hall of the temple is the porch in front of the sanctum?
questions.explanation: The pyramidal-roofed Jagamohana, the audience hall in front of the sanctum, is the largest surviving part of the temple.

questions.q: Near which famous Odisha pilgrimage town is Konark located, completing the 'Golden Triangle' of the state?
questions.explanation: Konark lies near Puri; Puri, Konark and Bhubaneswar form Odisha's tourist Golden Triangle.

questions.q: What famous classical dance festival is held annually with the Konark temple as its backdrop?
questions.explanation: The annual Konark Dance Festival celebrates Indian classical dance against the backdrop of the temple.

questions.q: Konark's carvings include large free-standing animal sculptures; which pair famously flanks the temple?
questions.explanation: Monumental sculptures of lions crushing elephants, among other animal figures, guard the temple.

questions.q: Konark is renowned, like Khajuraho, for what category of detailed surface carvings?
questions.explanation: The temple walls are covered with elaborate carvings, including erotic mithuna couples typical of the period.

questions.q: The poet Rabindranath Tagore famously wrote which line about Konark?
options: "A teardrop on the cheek of time"
"Here the language of stone surpasses the language of man"
questions.explanation: Tagore wrote that at Konark "the language of stone surpasses the language of man," praising its sculpture.

questions.q: Roughly how high is the surviving Jagamohana (porch) of the Konark temple?
questions.explanation: The pyramidal Jagamohana rises to about 30 metres (roughly 100 feet), the tallest surviving portion.

questions.q: The name 'Konark' is derived from Sanskrit words meaning what?
questions.explanation: Konark combines 'kona' (corner/angle) and 'arka' (sun), an epithet of Surya worshipped at this site.

questions.q: The original temple was oriented so that the rising sun would strike which part of the structure?
options: The entrance and image of Surya in the sanctum
questions.explanation: The temple faces east so the rising sun's rays would illuminate the entrance and the deity in the sanctum.

questions.q: Konark shares its Kalinga architectural style most closely with which other Odishan temples?
options: The Chola temples of Tamil Nadu
The temples of Bhubaneswar such as Lingaraja
questions.explanation: Konark shares the Kalinga architectural idiom with the great temples of nearby Bhubaneswar, such as the Lingaraja.

questions.q: In the early 20th century, the British colonial administration largely filled the Jagamohana with sand and stone for what reason?
options: To stabilize the structure and prevent its collapse
questions.explanation: Around 1903 the porch was filled in to support its walls and prevent the leaning structure from collapsing.

questions.q: The Eastern Ganga king Narasimhadeva I is traditionally said to have built Konark to commemorate what?
options: His military victories over Muslim invaders from Bengal
questions.explanation: Tradition holds the temple commemorated Narasimhadeva I's military successes against invading Muslim forces from Bengal, notably his 1243 victory at Katasin over the Bengal governor Tughan Khan.

questions.q: Which Indian currency note has featured an image of the Konark Sun Temple's wheel?
questions.explanation: The Konark wheel appears on the reverse of the 10-rupee Indian banknote, reflecting its national iconic status.
